positioning.headline: Trust infrastructure for consequential AI and software systems
positioning.subheadline: Entraphy helps organizations determine whether trust was warranted, preserve the evidence behind that judgment, and verify it later across time, boundary, and challenge.

seo.title: Entraphy | Trust infrastructure for consequential AI and software systems
seo.description: Entraphy makes machine-speed trust provable with evidence-backed trust infrastructure, replayable proof, and portable verification.

sections.headline: Trust infrastructure for consequential AI and software systems
sections.subheadline: Entraphy helps organizations determine whether trust was warranted, preserve the evidence behind that judgment, and verify it later across time, boundary, and challenge.

items.title: Determine when trust is warranted
items.body: Entraphy evaluates trust-relevant conditions at the point of consequence, not only after something goes wrong.

items.title: Preserve proof at decision time
items.body: Entraphy captures whether an action was acceptable when it happened and preserves the evidence behind that judgment.

items.title: Make consequential decisions replayable
items.body: Entraphy preserves proof that supports later replay, reconstruction, and review.

items.title: Carry trust beyond the original system
items.body: Entraphy makes trust portable, durable, and independently verifiable across time, teams, and boundaries.

sections.headline: In consequential environments, trust is earned.
paragraphs: In high-stakes environments, trust is not a story you tell. It is a judgment you make under uncertainty using evidence.
You establish identity. You verify origin. You examine behavior. You compare independent signals. And you watch whether the story holds up over time.
Entraphy brings that same discipline into the digital world, where AI and autonomous systems now act faster than human verification can keep up.
sections.closingLine: We are not manufacturing certainty. We are determining when trust has actually been earned.

sections.headline: Entraphy makes machine-speed trust provable.
body: When AI or autonomous systems act, organizations often cannot answer basic questions: What happened? Was it allowed? What trust state existed at the moment of action? Can that be proven later to security, legal, regulators, or partners?
Entraphy exists to answer those questions with evidence.
At the point of consequence, Entraphy evaluates trust state, preserves proof, supports replay, and makes consequential decisions independently verifiable later.
This is not just observability. It is not just logging. And it is not a generic governance wrapper.
It is trust proof infrastructure for systems that can no longer rely on assumption.

sections.headline: Two product planes. One trust system.

cards.name: Trust Flight Recorder for AI
cards.tagline: Prove what happened. Prove it was allowed.
cards.body: The proof layer for AI-native systems. Preserve legality at decision time, seal witnesses, replay consequential actions, and export portable trust artifacts.

cards.name: Blacksmith
cards.tagline: Continuously harden what must be trusted.
cards.body: The autonomous hardening engine for modern software. Pressure boundaries, validate what is real, repair what is admissible, and preserve witness-grade hardening evidence over time.

sections.headline: Evidence first. Decision second. Proof always.

steps.title: Observe trust-relevant conditions
steps.body: Entraphy gathers the signals that matter at the point of consequence — the evidence that helps determine whether an action should proceed.

steps.title: Evaluate what is admissible
steps.body: Entraphy computes trust state, applies policy, and determines whether the action should proceed, be constrained, be held for review, or be stopped.

steps.title: Preserve what matters
steps.body: Entraphy preserves proof records that support later replay, investigation, and independent verification.

sections.headline: Built to work with the systems you already trust
sections.body: Entraphy is designed to sit alongside your existing observability, workflow, AI, and control systems. It does not require centralizing all telemetry. It does not become a runtime bottleneck. And it does not replace your current stack. Instead, Entraphy adds a trust-state and proof layer around selected consequential decision points.
bullets: Proof objects that can flow back into existing tools

sections.headline: Not another logging tool. Not another AI governance wrapper.
leftItems: track model behavior after the fact
rightItems: proves whether actions were trusted and legal when they occurred
captures trust state at decision time
sections.footer: Entraphy focuses on provable trust, not just monitoring or policy documentation.

sections.headline: Built for environments where later defensibility matters

cards.title: AI-native systems
cards.body: Prove and replay consequential actions taken by copilots, agents, or automated workflows.

cards.title: Security and incident response
cards.body: Preserve evidence that can support investigation, defensibility, and post-incident reconstruction.

cards.title: Regulated environments
cards.body: Carry trust artifacts into audits, oversight, and review without forcing crude centralization.

cards.title: Sovereign and cross-boundary systems
cards.body: Preserve portable proof that remains meaningful outside the original runtime.

cards.title: Autonomous and industrial systems
cards.body: Capture trust state at the point of action in environments where consequence is real and hard to reverse.

sections.headline: Entraphy is not a dashboard. It is a stability instrument.
body: Traditional interfaces show alerts, logs, and panels. Entraphy is designed to make the stability of intelligent systems visible, navigable, and provable.

It shows causality before metrics. It separates evidence, trust, control, execution, and proof. And it is built so a user should understand the system’s condition in seconds, not after hunting through screens.

sections.headline: When systems act, trust should not be assumed.
sections.subheadline: Entraphy helps organizations turn trust into evidence — and evidence into action.

articles.title: What Is Entraphy?
articles.summary: Entraphy is a trust proof layer for consequential AI and software systems.

blocks.headline: What this is
paragraphs: Entraphy is a trust proof layer for consequential AI and software systems. It helps organizations determine whether an important action was acceptable when it happened, preserve the evidence behind that decision, and verify it later across time, teams, and boundaries.
In plain English: Entraphy turns trust into evidence.

callout.title: What output the customer gets
callout.body: Preserved proof artifacts, replayable evidence, trust-state outcomes, and the ability to support later review or independent verification.

blocks.headline: Why it matters
paragraphs: Most organizations can observe that something happened. Far fewer can prove whether it should have happened.

That gap becomes dangerous when AI systems, agents, workflows, or automated controls begin to act in ways that are consequential, sensitive, expensive, or hard to reverse. When something goes wrong, leaders need a different class of answer: what did the system do, was it allowed at the moment it acted, what trust state existed at that moment, and what evidence supported the decision?

blocks.headline: The problem it solves
paragraphs: Existing tools are useful, but they usually solve adjacent problems.

Observability platforms show telemetry. SIEMs collect security events. Model monitoring tracks performance and drift. Governance tools document policy and oversight. Entraphy sits in a different place. It focuses on consequential decision paths and preserves proof that an action was trusted and acceptable under the conditions that existed when it occurred.

blocks.headline: How it works
paragraphs: At a high level, Entraphy gathers trust-relevant signals, evaluates trust state, applies policy, determines whether the action should proceed, and preserves a proof artifact for later review.
That means Entraphy is concerned with evidence, admissibility, and defensibility, not just visibility.

blocks.headline: Where it fits in the stack
paragraphs: Entraphy is designed to work alongside existing systems rather than replace them. It adds a trust-state and proof layer around important decision points.

blocks.headline: What it is not
paragraphs: Entraphy is not just logging, not just observability, not just a governance dashboard, and not just another AI monitoring tool.

blocks.headline: Example workflow
paragraphs: An AI system proposes an action. Entraphy evaluates the trust-relevant conditions around that action, applies policy, determines whether it should proceed, and preserves proof of the decision. Later, a reviewer can replay and examine what happened.

articles.title: How Entraphy Works
articles.summary: A simple trust checkpoint flow: gather signals, evaluate trust state, apply policy, preserve proof.

blocks.headline: What this is
paragraphs: This page explains Entraphy’s core operating pattern in simple terms.

blocks.headline: The end-to-end flow
paragraphs: Entraphy works as a trust checkpoint on consequential decision paths.

blocks.headline: Why this matters
paragraphs: The most important difference is timing.

Most systems explain events after they occur. Entraphy is designed to evaluate and preserve proof at the point of consequence. That is why Entraphy can help answer not only what happened, but whether the action was acceptable when it happened.

blocks.headline: What output the customer gets
paragraphs: The result can include a proof artifact, replayable evidence, trust-state outcome, and supporting materials for later review or portability.

blocks.headline: Where it fits in the stack
paragraphs: Entraphy is typically applied to selected high-value or high-risk decision points, not to every event in an environment.

blocks.headline: What it is not
paragraphs: It is not a giant centralization project, not a generic inline bottleneck, and not an after-the-fact reporting layer.

blocks.headline: Example workflow
paragraphs: A workflow engine proposes a high-consequence action. Entraphy evaluates trust-relevant evidence, applies policy, produces an outcome, and preserves proof for replay and review.

articles.title: Why Proof at Decision Time Matters
articles.summary: Decision-time proof closes the gap between what happened and what was allowed.

blocks.headline: What this is
paragraphs: This page explains Entraphy’s core wedge: decision-time proof.

blocks.headline: Why it matters
paragraphs: When organizations investigate a consequential event, they usually start with logs, dashboards, tickets, or scattered telemetry. Those tools may show pieces of what happened, but they often fail to prove whether the action was acceptable when it occurred.
If a system makes an important decision, the real question is not only what happened. It is whether that action was allowed under the conditions that existed at that exact moment.

blocks.headline: The problem it solves
paragraphs: Retrospective evidence is often incomplete, noisy, or hard to defend.

Logs can be missing context. Observability may show symptoms without legality. Governance tools may show policy without proving runtime adherence. Model monitoring may show drift without telling you whether a specific action should have proceeded. Decision-time proof closes that gap.

blocks.headline: How it works
paragraphs: Entraphy evaluates trust-relevant conditions before or at the moment an action occurs, applies policy, and preserves the resulting proof.
This creates a defensible record that supports replay, review, and later verification.

blocks.headline: What output the customer gets
paragraphs: Customers gain stronger defensibility, replayable evidence, and a preserved answer to whether an action was acceptable when it happened.

blocks.headline: What it is not
paragraphs: Decision-time proof is not just a better log. It is proof created at the moment of consequence.

blocks.headline: Example workflow
paragraphs: A customer asks why an AI-driven action was allowed. Instead of reconstructing the event from fragments, the team can review the preserved proof, trust state, and policy outcome associated with that decision.

articles.title: Entraphy in Your Existing Stack
articles.summary: Entraphy works alongside observability, SIEM, workflow engines, AI runtimes, and governance systems.

blocks.headline: What this is
paragraphs: This page explains where Entraphy fits relative to the tools a customer already has.

blocks.headline: Why it matters
paragraphs: One of the most common objections is fear of rip-and-replace. Buyers want to know whether Entraphy requires them to centralize all telemetry, replace their observability stack, or accept a new control bottleneck.
The answer is no.

blocks.headline: Where Entraphy sits
paragraphs: Entraphy is designed to work alongside the systems customers already trust for telemetry, operations, and execution.

blocks.headline: How it works with existing systems
paragraphs: Entraphy ingests relevant evidence from surrounding systems, normalizes it into trust-relevant inputs, evaluates consequential decisions, and emits proof artifacts, trust outcomes, and replayable records.
That means customers keep the systems they already trust for telemetry, operations, and execution while Entraphy adds defensible proof around what matters most.

blocks.headline: What output the customer gets
paragraphs: Customers get bounded evidence, proof artifacts, replay support, and stronger defensibility without forcing crude raw-data gravity.

blocks.headline: What it is not
paragraphs: Entraphy is not meant to replace Splunk, Datadog, OpenTelemetry, workflow systems, or every control surface in the environment.

blocks.headline: Example workflow
paragraphs: A customer keeps their existing observability and AI runtime stack. Entraphy is added to one high-value decision path to preserve legality-at-decision and portable proof.

articles.title: Proof Pilot Overview
articles.summary: A fixed-scope pilot for one meaningful workflow or decision surface.

blocks.headline: What this is
paragraphs: The Proof Pilot is the standard starting point for customers.

blocks.headline: Why it matters
paragraphs: Entraphy is easiest to understand when it is applied to one real workflow. The pilot gives customers a bounded way to evaluate fit, see actual proof outputs, and validate whether the system solves a meaningful evidence problem.

blocks.headline: What the pilot is
paragraphs: The Entraphy Proof Pilot is a fixed-scope pilot for one production-like AI workflow, agent, service, or high-value decision surface.

blocks.headline: Pilot scope
paragraphs: A typical pilot is designed to stay narrow so the evidence stays real and the next step stays easy to understand.
bullets: Generate legality proofs at decision time
Show trust evidence for selected actions
Demonstrate later verification outside the originating runtime

blocks.headline: Success criteria
paragraphs: A successful pilot should show that Entraphy can prove what happened, prove whether it was allowed, show the trust state at the point of action, reconstruct at least one action or incident path, and produce a trust artifact that remains meaningful later.

blocks.headline: Pilot outputs
paragraphs: Customers should leave the pilot with a pilot summary, evidence examples, one replay walkthrough, one portable proof artifact, and a recommendation for production rollout.

blocks.headline: What it is not
paragraphs: The Proof Pilot is not a giant enterprise transformation. It is a bounded engagement designed to demonstrate value quickly and credibly.

articles.title: FAQ
articles.summary: Answers to the most common questions about observability, logging, governance, and proof.

entries.question: Is Entraphy just observability?
entries.answer: No. Observability helps you understand what happened in a system. Entraphy focuses on whether a consequential action was acceptable when it happened, and on preserving proof that supports later replay and independent verification.

entries.question: Is Entraphy just logging?
entries.answer: No. Logs are usually retrospective. Entraphy is built to evaluate trust-relevant conditions at decision time and preserve proof that supports later review.

entries.question: Is Entraphy governance software?
entries.answer: Not in the usual sense. It is not a generic policy wrapper or documentation layer. It operates on consequential decision paths and preserves proof that can be reviewed, replayed, and independently verified.

entries.question: Does Entraphy replace our existing stack?
entries.answer: No. Entraphy works alongside observability, SIEM, workflow systems, AI tooling, and existing controls. It adds a trust-state and proof layer around important decisions.

entries.question: Does Entraphy require centralizing all telemetry?
entries.answer: No. Entraphy is designed around bounded evidence and compact artifacts, not raw-data gravity.

entries.question: Does Entraphy become a bottleneck?
entries.answer: Not in the way buyers usually fear. Entraphy is intended to sit on selected high-value decision points, not on every event in the environment.

entries.question: What does decision-time proof mean?
entries.answer: It means evaluating trust-relevant conditions before or at the moment an action occurs, applying policy, and preserving proof that supports later review.

entries.question: What is a trust checkpoint?
entries.answer: A trust checkpoint is the point where Entraphy evaluates whether a consequential action should proceed, produces an outcome, and preserves proof.

entries.question: What is replay?
entries.answer: Replay is the ability to revisit a preserved decision and examine the evidence and outcome later.

entries.question: What are sealed witnesses?
entries.answer: Sealed witnesses are preserved evidence records associated with an important decision. In many customer conversations, it is simpler to introduce them as preserved proof records first.

entries.question: What makes proof portable?
entries.answer: Portable proof remains meaningful outside the original runtime or environment and can support later review or verification.

entries.question: What is independent verification?
entries.answer: Independent verification means someone outside the original decision path can examine the proof and assess what happened.

entries.question: How do customers usually start?
entries.answer: The standard starting point is the Proof Pilot.

articles.title: Glossary
articles.summary: The core terms used across the Entraphy site and documentation.

terms.definition: An action that is allowed to proceed because the relevant conditions support it at the time of decision.
An action important enough that an organization may need stronger control, evidence, and later defensibility around it.
Proof created by evaluating trust-relevant conditions before or at the moment an action occurs, then preserving the result for later review.
The ability for a party outside the original decision path to examine proof and assess what happened.
The rules or conditions used to determine whether an action should proceed.
Proof that remains meaningful outside the original runtime or environment.
The preserved output of a trust-relevant decision, including the evidence and outcome needed to support later review.
A bounded engagement focused on applying Entraphy to one meaningful workflow or decision path to demonstrate fit and value.
The ability to revisit a preserved decision path and examine what happened later.
A preserved evidence record associated with a consequential decision.
How Entraphy works alongside existing tools and systems rather than replacing them.
The point where Entraphy evaluates evidence, applies policy, determines whether an action should proceed, and preserves proof.
A high-level description of Entraphy: a layer that helps prove consequential decisions were acceptable under the conditions that existed at the time.
Any input, context, or evidence source that helps determine whether an action should proceed.
The evaluated condition derived from trust-relevant inputs at decision time.

companyArticles.title: What Is Entraphy?
companyArticles.summary: Entraphy is a trust infrastructure platform for consequential AI and software systems.

blocks.headline: What this is
paragraphs: Entraphy is a trust infrastructure platform for consequential AI and software systems. It helps organizations determine whether trust was warranted, preserve the evidence behind that judgment, and verify it later across time, teams, and boundaries.

In plain terms: Entraphy turns trust into evidence. Entraphy is the company and platform. Its products include Trust Flight Recorder for AI, the proof product, and Blacksmith, the autonomous hardening product.

blocks.headline: Why it matters
paragraphs: Most organizations can observe AI or software activity, but still cannot reliably answer the harder questions after something consequential happens: What did the system do? Was it allowed? What trust state existed at that moment? Can we still prove it later?
Entraphy exists to close that gap between visibility and verifiability.

blocks.headline: The problem it solves
paragraphs: Existing tools usually solve adjacent problems. Observability platforms show telemetry. SIEMs collect events. Model monitoring tracks behavior. Governance tools document process.

Entraphy focuses on decision-time legality, trust state, replayable evidence, and portable proof. It is positioned as a system of record and proof substrate, not just another dashboard, gateway, or monitoring product.

companyArticles.title: How Entraphy Works
companyArticles.summary: Signals, trust state, policy, action, and proof.

blocks.headline: The operating model
paragraphs: This page explains the Entraphy platform operating model, not just one product feature.
Buyers need to understand Entraphy as a control-and-proof system, not just a monitoring tool. The core model is: signals → trust state → policy → action → proof.

blocks.headline: Why this matters
paragraphs: Most systems explain events after they occur. Entraphy is designed to evaluate and preserve proof at the point of consequence, so later review is grounded in what was true when the action happened, not just in what logs survived later.

blocks.headline: What output or artifact the customer gets
paragraphs: Depending on the flow, the customer can receive Trust Flight Recorder records, Proof Receipts, replayable evidence, trust-state outcomes, and portable verification artifacts.

companyArticles.title: Entraphy in Your Existing Stack
companyArticles.summary: Entraphy sits beside observability, SIEM, workflow engines, and AI runtimes.

blocks.headline: Why buyers care
paragraphs: One of the biggest objections is fear of rip-and-replace. Customers want to know whether Entraphy requires them to centralize all telemetry, replace Splunk, Datadog, or OpenTelemetry, or accept a new runtime bottleneck. The answer is no.

blocks.headline: Where Entraphy sits
paragraphs: Organizations already have telemetry, logs, and controls, but they often lack a defensible proof layer around consequential actions.

blocks.headline: How it works
paragraphs: Entraphy ingests bounded evidence from surrounding systems, normalizes those inputs into canonical evidence objects, evaluates trust-relevant decisions, and emits proof artifacts, trust outcomes, and replayable records. It is designed around bounded evidence and compact artifacts, not raw-data gravity.

companyArticles.title: Products Overview
companyArticles.summary: Entraphy, Trust Flight Recorder for AI, and Blacksmith stay distinct.

relatedPages.title: What Is Trust Flight Recorder for AI?

blocks.headline: Three layers, clear roles
paragraphs: Entraphy is the company and trust-proof platform.
Trust Flight Recorder for AI is the proof product.
Blacksmith is the autonomous hardening product.

blocks.headline: How they differ
paragraphs: Trust Flight Recorder for AI proves what happened, whether it was allowed, and what evidence supported the decision.
Blacksmith pressures boundaries, validates what is real, repairs what is admissible, and remembers how the system hardened over time.

blocks.headline: How they work together
paragraphs: Blacksmith hardens. Entraphy proves.

relatedPages.title: What Is Trust Flight Recorder for AI?

blocks.headline: The usual starting point
paragraphs: Customers usually do not start with the full platform all at once. They start with one meaningful workflow, one decision surface, or one trust problem that is painful enough to justify proof.

blocks.headline: Typical next steps
paragraphs: Some teams start with the company platform view, some start with Trust Flight Recorder for AI, and some start with Blacksmith.

For many, the first commercial wedge is Trust Flight Recorder for AI because it shows legality proof at decision time, replayable evidence, portable proof artifacts, and independent verification outside the original runtime.

blocks.headline: What this is
paragraphs: Trust Flight Recorder for AI is Entraphy’s proof product. It preserves a cryptographically verifiable timeline of trust history so organizations can prove what happened, whether it was allowed, what trust state existed at the point of action, and whether that proof still holds later.
The approved shorthand is: the black box recorder for trust.

blocks.headline: What it solves
paragraphs: AI systems and agents can act faster than teams can review them. That creates a gap between observability and defensibility.
Trust Flight Recorder for AI closes that gap by preserving proof at decision time.

paragraphs: Blacksmith is the autonomous hardening product line inside Entraphy.
It is a governed hardening engine, not a passive detector.

blocks.headline: What it solves
paragraphs: Software and AI-adjacent systems need more than detection. They need a hardening loop that pressures boundaries, validates what is real, repairs what is safe to repair, and leaves evidence behind.

blocks.headline: What it gives the customer
paragraphs: Campaign memory, regression evidence, repair history, and durable proof of how the system hardened.

blacksmithArticles.title: The Hardening Loop
blacksmithArticles.summary: Pressure, challenge, validate, govern, repair, verify, witness, remember.

blocks.headline: Sequence
paragraphs: Pressure. Challenge. Validate. Govern. Repair. Verify. Witness. Remember.

blocks.headline: Why it matters
paragraphs: The loop turns hardening into a repeatable campaign instead of a one-off patch cycle.

blacksmithArticles.title: What Blacksmith Is Not
blacksmithArticles.summary: Not a scanner, patch bot, generic AppSec wrapper, or autonomous pentest toy.

blocks.headline: Not these things
paragraphs: Blacksmith is not a scanner, not a patch bot, not a generic AppSec wrapper, and not an autonomous pentest toy.

blocks.headline: What it is
paragraphs: It is a governed autonomous hardening system with replayable evidence and compounding learning.

blocks.headline: The relationship
paragraphs: Blacksmith is the implementation-pressure and remediation system. Entraphy is the trust, inquiry, and proof substrate. Blacksmith attacks and hardens. Entraphy records, proves, and exports the evidence.
Blacksmith hardens. Entraphy proves.

blocks.headline: Why this boundary matters
paragraphs: Keeping them separate preserves clean architectural boundaries while making the trust system stronger over time.

rules: no doctrine wall above the fold
